Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's explosive 29-ball 94 powers India A to tri-series title
description: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's blistering 94 off 29 balls powered India A to a 66-run victory over Sri Lanka A in the tri-series final. The teenage sensation's record-breaking half-century set the stage for a massive total of 377. Despite a spirited chase from Sri Lanka A, strong bowling from Yash Thakur and Vipraj Nigam secured the championship for India A.

UP horror: Drunk 14-year-old boy rapes 9-month-old in Gorakhpur
description: A nine-month-old girl was allegedly abducted and sexually assaulted by her 14-year-old maternal uncle in Gorakhpur. The accused, who claimed to be under the influence of alcohol, reportedly took the infant from her home while she slept. The baby was later found injured in a field. Police apprehended the juvenile after reviewing CCTV footage and collecting evidence, and he has been sent to a shelter home for further legal proceedings.

Massive fire breaks out at private company in Noida Sector 9, firefighting underway
description: A large-scale fire incident occurred at a private establishment in Noida Sector 9 on Sunday, leading to a rapid deployment by Fire and Emergency Services. Firefighters quickly moved in to control the situation, with officials ensuring that no one was trapped. Confidence remains high for a prompt resolution, and investigations are currently being conducted to uncover the cause and evaluate the damage incurred.

Message for US? China unveils first Dongfeng-17 hypersonic missile launch footage
description: China's state broadcaster CCTV has revealed footage of the Dongfeng-17 hypersonic missile launch, a significant display of Beijing's enhanced military capabilities. Analysts suggest this move bolsters China's deterrent posture within the crucial first island chain. The missile, capable of speeds exceeding Mach 5, poses a formidable challenge to interception, reaching targets across vital strategic waterways. This demonstration underscores China's commitment to national security and its advanced missile development.

‘Fund crunch, caustic remarks’: Uddhav camp MP confirms crossover to ruling Sena
description: A Shiv Sena (UBT) Lok Sabha MP, Nagesh Patil Ashtikar, has switched to the Eknath Shinde faction, citing a need for development funds for his constituency. He stated that the limited MPLADS fund was insufficient to address public expectations. Ashtikar also alluded to "certain remarks" made by a senior Sena (UBT) leader as a catalyst for his decision, suggesting a lack of respect within the party.

Karnataka BJP turns to Lord Manjunatha to uncover truth in cross-voting row
description: Karnataka BJP is investigating alleged cross-voting by its MLAs in the recent Legislative Council polls, which saw the ruling Congress secure a significant victory. Party president B Y Vijayendra announced a legislature party meeting at Dharmasthala, where MLAs might take an oath before Lord Manjunatha to reveal the truth. He termed the alleged betrayal a serious matter that will be pursued to its conclusion, aiming to restore party worker confidence.

title: Watch: Pradhan talks to parents of NEET aspirant appearing after surgery
description: Union Minister Dharmendra Pradhan spoke with the parents of Shrishti Dubey, a Kolkata NEET-UG aspirant who, despite a severe road accident and surgery, was able to take the re-exam. Special medical arrangements, including a separate room and ambulance, were made following government intervention. Shrishti's parents expressed immense gratitude, crediting the minister for enabling their daughter's participation. Pradhan conveyed his best wishes for her success and future as a doctor.

title: Shah Rukh Khan, Dharmendra saved Comedy Nights with Kapil from early cancellation

UGC NET June 2026: Exam begins tomorrow; check reporting time, documents required
Map, hunt, kill: Inside India's powerful trishul for networked naval warfare
description: India's Navy is enhancing its maritime warfare capabilities by integrating new vessels into a cohesive network. INS Dunagiri, a stealth frigate, INS Sanshodhak, a survey ship, and INS Agray, an anti-submarine craft, were commissioned, showcasing a strategy focused on layered awareness and combat power. This integrated approach aims to bolster India's security in the increasingly active Indian Ocean.

title: Breaking Bad' star Giancarlo Esposito accepts Islam after visit to Saudi Arabia
description: Acclaimed actor Giancarlo Esposito, famous for 'Breaking Bad' and 'The Boys', has embraced Islam. He reportedly recited the Shahada, the declaration of faith, while filming his latest project, '7 Dogs', in Saudi Arabia. The news was shared by Turki Alalshikh, Chairman of Saudi Arabia’s General Entertainment Authority, via social media, accompanied by a video of Esposito praying.

title: Karnataka BJP MLAs to meet at Dharmasthala temple to identify cross-voters
Indian women’s hockey team wins FIH Nations Cup, earn Pro League promotion
TV presenter quits after falsely announcing Messi's father's death live on air
description: A TV presenter has resigned after mistakenly announcing Lionel Messi's father's death on live television. Florencia Pena shared the false news, claiming it was verified by her show's production team. Luzu TV has terminated staff involved, emphasizing their commitment to responsible journalism. Messi's family confirmed his father is recovering from an undisclosed medical condition, following a triumphant World Cup opener for the star player.

title: 'Big names involved, govt may fall': Kejriwal on Ram Temple donation 'theft' case
description: Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) national convenor Arvind Kejriwal has alleged that "big names" are involved in the Ram Temple donation theft case, claiming the government could face collapse if their identities are revealed. He questioned the lack of FIRs despite reports of crores in cash and valuables being stolen, urging swift action to protect public faith over political interests.

Akhtar defended Meloni, questioning Trump's claims and his character. When a user brought religion into the debate, Akhtar firmly reiterated his atheist identity, stating his community is all Indians.

Why Starmer is facing fresh pressure to exit after Labour rival Andy Burnham’s victory
description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces intense pressure following Andy Burnham's return to Parliament, sparking leadership challenge speculation. Despite denials from senior ministers, Starmer is reportedly reflecting on his position amid Labour's recent electoral setbacks and declining approval. Burnham has signaled his ambition, warning the party needs a "final chance to change." Starmer, however, vows to contest any leadership bid, insisting he will not step aside voluntarily.

title: Sooryavanshi's potential India debut gets green signal after Cricket Ireland's big decision
description: Cricket Ireland has confirmed the upcoming T20I series against India will proceed as planned in Belfast on June 26 and 28. Following a security review with local authorities, officials are confident in the safety measures for players and spectators. This marks India's return to Stormont after nearly two decades, with anticipation building around potential senior debut for young sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi.

Bumrah returns for England ODIs: How many has he missed since 2023 WC?
description: Jasprit Bumrah has returned to India's ODI squad for the England series after being rested for the Afghanistan ODIs. Since the 2023 World Cup, the pace spearhead has missed eight ODI assignments as part of workload management. With India beginning a new World Cup cycle, Bumrah's return provides a major boost to the team's pace attack in English conditions.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn, makes 540 workers millionaires
description: A small-town US family has sold their Louisiana-based electrical-equipment company Fibrebond to power-management giant Eaton for $1.7 billion—and made sure their 540 full-time workers got a $240 million cut of it. Former CEO Graham Walker wrote a single clause into the deal: 15% of proceeds for the staff, none of whom held any equity. The average bonus works out to around $443,000 per worker.

PM Modi delays Delhi airport exit to ensure smooth commute for NEET-UG re-exam candidates
description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi delayed his departure from Delhi airport on Sunday to prevent traffic disruptions for students appearing for the NEET-UG re-exam. Arriving at 1:15 pm, he waited to ensure smooth passage to examination centres across 551 cities and 14 international locations. The exam, held at 5,440 centres, featured extensive CCTV surveillance and jammers to prevent malpractice, with heightened security and strict checks in Delhi.

title: From the Opinions Editor | Mind the pedestrian: Supreme Court’s call for walkable cities must be answered
India beat New Zealand 2-0 to clinch second FIH Women's Nations Cup
description: India defeated hosts New Zealand 2-0 in the final to win their second FIH Women's Nations Cup title and secure promotion to the FIH Women's Pro League. Goals from Navneet Kaur and Sunelita Toppo sealed the victory as India completed an unbeaten campaign. Lalremsiami was named Player of the Match, while Deepika finished as the tournament's joint-highest scorer with six goals.

title: India-EU FTA to open nearly entire European market for Indian exports by 2027: Goyal
description: India and the European Union are poised to sign their landmark free trade agreement by December, with implementation expected by early 2027. This pact promises Indian exporters near-zero duty access to the vast European market, opening up significant opportunities. Discussions are also progressing on trade deals with the US and Canada, highlighting India's growing global economic influence.

title: 'Hands tied, cloth gag in mouth': Man fakes own abduction, dials family for Rs 50 lakh
description: A Surat man, Jignesh Talaviya, has been apprehended after allegedly staging his own kidnapping. He reportedly sent a ransom message to his wife, demanding ₹50 lakh to settle share market losses. Utran police successfully traced him to a hotel in Godhra, leading to his arrest for misleading authorities and fabricating evidence. Talaviya, an accountant, had been missing since June 12.

title: Keir Starmer hit by resignation rumors as allies deny he is stepping down
'Absolutely makes zero sense': Ex-India pacer blasts Gambhir-led team management
description: Former India bowler Dodda Ganesh has slammed the team management for resting KL Rahul in the final ODI against Afghanistan, questioning the decision as Rahul had faced minimal deliveries.  Despite this, India secured a dominant 3-0 series victory, with Yashasvi Jaiswal scoring an unbeaten century and Prasidh Krishna taking a five-wicket haul. The win marks India's first ODI series triumph under captain Shubman Gill.

title: From SSC success to future goals: Transwoman Kajal Sharma’s journey at 51
Ranveer Singh's Rs 325 crore 'Dhurandhar' fee beats Shah Rukh Khan, Rajinikanth
description: Ranveer Singh has reportedly earned a staggering Rs 325 crore from his latest blockbuster, 'Dhurandhar.' This unprecedented figure, achieved through a profit-sharing deal rather than a flat fee, has placed him ahead of other top Indian stars. The film's massive success across various revenue streams, including digital and satellite rights, underscores the rewarding nature of his bold investment strategy.

title: Iran coach calls out World Cup’s 47 other managers: ‘I would say something’
'Exam doesn't matter, my burqa & identity do': NEET aspirant stopped at Ajmer exam centre
description: A NEET-UG re-examination candidate in Ajmer alleged she was prevented from entering the exam hall unless she removed her burqa and dupatta. Kulsum Bano stated she wore the same attire during a previous attempt and would refuse to take the test if her religious identity was challenged. The re-exam proceeded with stringent security measures nationwide following paper leak allegations.

NEET aspirant given Abu Dhabi centre takes re-test in Nagpur after NTA correction
description: A NEET-UG 2026 candidate from Nagpur, initially assigned an exam center in Abu Dhabi, successfully appeared for the re-test in his hometown after the NTA revised the location. The issue gained national attention, prompting swift action from the agency. While the NTA stated the city change was made via the candidate's login, the family maintained their preference for local centers. This incident occurred amidst the ongoing NEET-UG 2026 re-examination.

description: A Bengaluru tech professional's 12-year career journey, starting with a ₹5,000 monthly salary and no degree, has culminated in a ₹45 lakh annual package and an estimated ₹70 lakh net worth. Despite setbacks like a 2025 layoff, the individual's honest account of gradual growth, career breaks, and self-doubt resonates widely, highlighting the path to a high-paying tech role.

title: Before Le Corbusier: The Chandigarh that might have been
7 dead, over 40 hospitalised after ammonia leak at Tamil Nadu seafood factory
description: Tragedy struck a seafood processing plant in Tamil Nadu's Tiruvallur district on Sunday when an ammonia gas leak claimed seven lives and hospitalised over 40 individuals. Workers and others at the Manjankaranai facility reportedly collapsed after inhaling the toxic fumes. Emergency services swiftly responded, securing the area and working to control the leak. Those affected were rushed to nearby hospitals for urgent medical attention.

'Stand with us': With thalis in hand, CJP continues protest at Jantar Mantar
description: Members of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) continued their protest at Jantar Mantar, demanding Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over alleged examination irregularities. Despite police directives to vacate, the group, led by founder Abhijeet Dipke, vowed to stay until the minister resigns and justice is served for students. Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk also pledged support, threatening a hunger strike.

title: ‘Seriously 4 times’: Ratan Tata on falling in love multiple times and why it didn’t succeed; psychologist weighs in
Does your landlord want your Aadhaar, PAN or salary slips? Documents that you are required to share
description: Renting a house? Landlords often ask for extensive financial documents, but the Model Tenancy Act of 2021 clarifies tenant obligations. Only basic identification like Aadhaar and PAN cards are legally required for the Rent Authority. Financial history such as salary slips and bank statements are not mandatory under the Act, though landlords may use them for private vetting. Remember, states must adopt this framework for it to be legally binding.

19-year-old NEET aspirant dies by suicide in Hyderabad
description: A 19-year-old NEET aspirant tragically died by suicide in Hyderabad's Miyapur, police reported Sunday. Found hanging in her apartment, she left a note stating no one was responsible. Investigators suspect intense pressure from the NEET re-examination, scheduled for Sunday, may have led to this devastating outcome. The incident highlights the immense stress faced by students preparing for competitive exams.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: The boy monster who wakes up on big occasions
description: Sportspersons are often advised to play according to occasion, to minimise risk when the pressure rises. Sooryavanshi appears to have chosen a different route. Whether it was the World Cup final, the IPL knockout or Sunday's tri-series final, he has trusted the same game that brought him here in the first place. He has trusted the attack and his wrists.

Spanish PM’s wife banned from leaving country before trial
Anthropic co-founder who said software engineering is 'dead', now says days of AI prompts are over
description: AI pioneer Boris Cherny, co-founder of Anthropic, is shifting his focus from manual prompt writing to 'loop engineering.' This new approach involves AI agents autonomously generating and refining prompts, moving beyond constant human input.  Other AI leaders like Peter Steinberger and Addy Osmani echo this sentiment, emphasizing the design of these AI 'loops' over direct prompting. This evolution positions AI agents as more akin to employees, managing tasks and workflows.

'Amma, Appa, Anna, Sorry': Another NEET aspirant dies before retest in Tamil Nadu
description: A 20-year-old NEET aspirant in Hosur died by suicide on the eve of his retest, reportedly due to immense pressure and fear of failing after three years of preparation. His parents were away when he took the extreme step. A suicide note found expressed his love for his family and his deep-seated anxiety about the exam. Police are investigating the tragic incident.

Satya Nadella's new inner circle: Meet Microsoft's key leaders
description: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella has restructured the senior leadership team that ran Microsoft for decades, replacing it with leaner, startup-style teams built for the AI era. Business Insider reports the SLT is gone, swapped for a five-person corporate group, a 35-strong engineering team and a dedicated Copilot unit. Nadella now reviews AI metrics weekly. Longtime executives including Rajesh Jha, Yusuf Mehdi and Charlie Bell are out as Microsoft's AI reboot reshapes its power structure.

description: A Delhi techie's father has accused his son-in-law of murder following her death at a Mussoorie homestay. The father vehemently denies claims of his daughter being drunk, alleging she was drugged and killed. Police are investigating the suspicious death, with the victim's viscera sent for testing and a postmortem report awaited. The husband claims they consumed alcohol before he found her unconscious and bleeding.

Jilted lover stabs 5 in ex-girlfriend’s Telangana house after rejection
description: A jilted lover's pre-dawn knife attack left five family members injured in Hyderabad after he allegedly broke into his former girlfriend's home. The 22-year-old unemployed man, enraged by the breakup a year ago, reportedly climbed to the second-floor flat and attacked his ex-partner, her parents, sister, and uncle. Neighbours intervened, overpowering the accused who was subsequently arrested and remanded in judicial custody.

title: Rap, rhyme, satire: CJP’s second show of dissent in Delhi
description: Artists and young people gathered in Delhi for the Cockroach Janta Party's protest, using rap, poetry, and memes to criticize the NEET-UG paper leak and governance issues. Demanding the Union Education Minister's resignation, the group chanted slogans and performed songs. Despite tighter security and police requests to disperse, protesters continued their demonstration, vowing to persist until their demands are met.

Why India, among largest economies, doesn’t rank in top 50 world’s most powerful passports
description: Despite India's robust economic growth, its passport ranks surprisingly low globally, hovering around the 80th position. This reflects a slower expansion of visa-free access compared to other nations. Factors like diplomatic ties, reciprocal agreements, and perceived security influence rankings, not just economic might. India's progress is gradual, requiring strategic diplomatic efforts and strengthened internal systems to boost international trust and improve its travel freedom.

title: Happy Father’s Day 2026: Top 50 wishes, messages and quotes to make dad feel special
description: Fathers often express love through actions, not words, and Father's Day on June 21, 2026, is a time to appreciate their guidance and support. Simple gestures like cooking a favorite meal or sharing memories can mean more than gifts. This day is about acknowledging their sacrifices and letting them know they are deeply appreciated.
